domain.low: Monitor DNS and email reputation; no immediate action.
domain.medium: Add to watchlist; enable enhanced logging and alerting for this domain.
domain.high: Block on email/web gateways; alert SOC and investigate related traffic.
domain.critical: Immediate enterprise-wide block (DNS, proxies, email); search and eradicate; notify leadership.

ip.low: Allow but monitor for abnormal behavior; retain logs.
ip.medium: Rate-limit and monitor; consider temporary block if patterns persist.
ip.high: Block at firewall/WAF; review logs for related connections; notify incident response.
ip.critical: Immediate network-wide block; hunt for lateral movement and exfiltration.

email.low: No action beyond monitoring; educate user on phishing awareness.
email.medium: Flag for caution; enable additional verification for messages from this domain.
email.high: Notify affected users; enforce password change and MFA verification.
email.critical: Force credential reset, review recent activity, and notify potentially impacted users.

url.low: Allow with caution; scan in sandbox if accessed.
url.medium: Quarantine and detonate in sandbox; warn users.
url.high: Block at proxies and email; submit for takedown if applicable.
url.critical: Immediate block and purge; distribute IOC; initiate takedown processes.

password_sha1.medium: Discourage use; prompt for change at next login.
password_sha1.critical: Treat as compromised; force reset and invalidate sessions immediately.

password_prefix.low: Prefix not widely seen; no immediate action.
password_prefix.high: Disallow passwords that hash to this prefix; notify security engineering.
password_prefix.critical: Treat as high-risk pattern; enforce immediate rotation and add to password blocklist.

notes: Map numeric risk_score to risk_level: critical >= 85; high >= 60; medium >= 30; low < 30. Apply recommendations by indicator type and derived level. Use demo/test mode for external checks when supported.
